title: salix × vollmannii
intro:
Salix × vollmannii, also known as the Vollmann willow or hybrid willow, is a hybrid plant species resulting from the cross between the Salix viminalis (white willow) and Salix alba (white willow), and is characterized by its attractive, deciduous foliage and robust growth habit.

This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include the ability to reduce inflammation and fever, as well as alleviate headaches and joint pain. The health benefits of Salix × vollmannii include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce stress, and improve overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of the plant are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
